perturbation.original_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, regardless if at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition that verbally disclosed information needs to be identified *at the time of disclosure* as confidential, all verbal information becomes automatically classified as 'Confidential Information'. This contradicts Section 7 which states that information that was already known by the Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by a legal and legitimate way, not subject to any obligation to be kept confidential, shall not be considered Confidential Information. Now, if the information was disclosed verbally without being identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, but was previously known to the receiving party, it's unclear if it's considered Confidential Information or not, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not include info rmation which is: a. The information was already known by the Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by a legal and legitimate way, not subject to any obligation to be kept confid ential; b. There has been prior written consent of Disclosing Party , to release the obligation of secrecy and confidentiality; c. Lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party without restrictions as to use and disclose ; d. If the Receiving Party receives a court order to disclose Confidential Information . In this case Receiving Party shall (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party in order to allow the latter to act and avoid such disclosure, and (ii) upon request, but provided that it complies with applicable Law, cooperate with the Disclosing Party in preventing such a disclosure; e. Publicly available through lawful disclosure; or f. Independently developed by or for the Receiving Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that verbally disclosed information must be identified as confidential *at the time of disclosure* to be considered 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, specifically section a, which states that information already known by the Receiving Party prior to disclosure, not subject to confidentiality, is not Confidential Information. Now, if the information was disclosed verbally without being identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, but was previously known to the receiving party, the contract is contradictory about whether it's considered Confidential Information or not. This ambiguity directly aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , regardless being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition that the information must be 'subject to the rules of intellectual property' to be considered confidential, the scope of confidential information expands. This contradicts the definition in Section 2 and 3, which implicitly suggests that Confidential Information is tied to trade secrets, know-how, designs etc. that typically fall under intellectual property. Now, regular business information not related to intellectual property could be argued as confidential, causing confusion with the term 'Information' in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition that confidential information must be 'subject to the rules of intellectual property.' This broadens the scope of what can be considered confidential. Section 2, which defines 'Information,' includes trade secrets, know-how, designs, etc., implying a connection to intellectual property. By removing this connection, the changed text creates a contradiction because regular business information not related to intellectual property could now be argued as confidential, conflicting with the intended scope of 'Information' as outlined in Section 2. This falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, as the meaning of 'Confidential Information' becomes inconsistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence);
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to any people;
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'the minimum required number of people' to 'any people,' the Receiving Party is now forbidden from disclosing Confidential Information to anyone, including employees, directors, officers, legal counsels, etc. This directly conflicts with the parenthetical clause which assumes that disclosures will occur to at least *some* people who are 'personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence'. The in-text contradiction makes the clause unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to any people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence);
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by restricting disclosure of confidential information to 'any people'. The original text allowed for disclosure to 'the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence)'. The changed text creates a direct conflict with the parenthetical clause of the contradicted text, as it explicitly prohibits disclosure to anyone, including those mentioned in the parenthetical clause. This is an Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Termination of any agreement executed by and between the Par ties, without any obligation to compensation for the complainant, being due, however, the penal ties for breach of contract by the party which gives cause to termination;
perturbation.changed_text: Termination of any agreement executed by and between the Par ties, with obligation to compensation for the complainant, being due, however, the penal ties for breach of contract by the party which gives cause to termination;
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'without any obligation to compensation for the complainant' to 'with obligation to compensation for the complainant,' directly contradicts the phrase which indicates that penalties for breach of contract will be applied to the party that gives cause to termination of the agreement. Therefore, now there is ambiguity about which penalty will be applied. This creates an **internal contradiction** within the same clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The breach of the terms s et forth herein imply: 2 de 2 PromonLogicalis Non-Disclosure Agreement (version August 2015) a. Termination of any agreement executed by and between the Par ties, with obligation to compensation for the complainant, being due, however, the penal ties for breach of contract by the party which gives cause to termination;
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'without any obligation to compensation for the complainant' to 'with obligation to compensation for the complainant'. This directly contradicts the phrase 'the penalties for breach of contract by the party which gives cause to termination'. Now there is a contradiction about which penalty will be applied (compensation vs. breach of contract penalties) creating an in-text contradiction within the same clause which is also where the contradicted text is located. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions).
perturbation.changed_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Receiving Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Receiving Party', the clause now states that all information furnished under the agreement, including confidential information, becomes the property of the *receiving* party. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 2 and 4.2, which clearly state that Confidential Information is the 'restricted property of the Disclosing Party' and the technical specifications as well as all documents to be disclosed remain the property of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the ownership of 'all information' furnished under the agreement from the 'Disclosing Party' to the 'Receiving Party'. The contradicted text states that 'Confidential Information' is the 'restricted property of the Disclosing Party'. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text now implies that the Receiving Party owns all information, including Confidential Information, conflicting with the contradicted text which states the Disclosing Party retains ownership of the Confidential Information.
